headline: Strategy sells $2.5M in bitcoin, Berkshire to acquire Taylor Morrison
summary: Market Catalysts Host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Markets and Data Editor Jared Blikre track several of the day's top trending stock tickers, including Strategy (MSTR) stock taking a hit after selling $2.5 million worth of bitcoin (BTC-USD), IBM (IBM) stock jumping on a resurfaced video of President Trump praising IBM CEO Arvind Krishna, and Berkshire Hathaway (BRK-A, BRK-B) to acquire Taylor Morrison Home (TMHC) in a $6..8 billion deal.

headline: What past tech waves can teach us about AI market leadership
summary: Investing.com -- In a note to clients on Monday, UBS drew on the history of past technology cycles to outline three principles it believes can help investors navigate the current AI rally, cautioning that today's leaders may not be tomorrow's and that hardware-focused players face the greatest risk when the current investment boom eventually fades.

headline: IBM Stock Adds To Quantum-Powered Rally. Analyst Says Don't Overlook Software Growth.
summary: IBM stock jumped in morning trading, adding oomph to a big rally that started with a $1 billion award from the federal government for quantum computing late last month.  On Monday, analysts with Barclays initiated coverage of IBM stock with a buy rating.  IBM stock climbed more than 8% to 323.74 in morning trades on the stock market today.

headline: Quantinuum Upsizes IPO. The Year’s Biggest Quantum Offering Is Getting Even Bigger.
summary: Quantinuum is set to make the biggest trading debut for any quantum company this year.  As anticipation rises, the company is upsizing its initial public offering to as much as $1.46 billion.  Quantinuum indicated Monday that it plans to offer 26.5 million shares at prices ranging from $53 to $55 each, compared with earlier plans to sell roughly 21 million shares for $45 to $50 each.
